items.headline: Eight new homes proposed on Wolfe Road lot
items.summary: A developer is seeking approval to demolish a single-family home at 781 S Wolfe Road and build eight two-story single-family dwellings on the 0.69-acre site, with a floor area ratio of 65.8%. The project also requires subdivision of the land into nine lots.

items.headline: 136-unit apartment complex permitted at Trimble
items.summary: Trimble Modular Apartments receives permit for 136 units plus parking at 110 Yoshino Way, totaling $26.9 million in construction. The project adds housing capacity in the north San José area near the Trimble tech corridor.

items.headline: Four townhomes planned at historic Crescent Avenue property
items.summary: A resource alteration permit is under review to develop four three-story townhomes on the rear of 434 Crescent Avenue while retaining the existing 960-square-foot historic house on the front. The hearing has been continued to July 1, 2026.

items.headline: Los Gatos schedules November 2026 municipal election
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for November 3, 2026, to fill three council seats. This is the regular election cycle for the city.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution approving the final subdivision map for Tract 10675 located on Boulevard. This clears the way for the project to proceed to construction or sale.

items.headline: 68-unit apartment building permitted on East St John
items.summary: New apartment complex with parking garage clears to build at 700 E St John St for $10.1 million. The project adds mid-sized rental housing in central San José.

items.headline: Community Development Block Grant funding planned for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Cupertino City Council is considering how to allocate federal Community Development Block Grant funds for the coming fiscal year. The agenda cuts off before details are provided, but CDBG funds typically support affordable housing, infrastructure, and community services.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan adopted
items.summary: The City Council adopted a new Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan at a study session on May 26. The plan sets direction for parks and recreation services in Mountain View.
